covered_sentences: The curate stopped coming to visit.
Nobody decent came near Wuthering Heights except Edgar Linton, who came to visit Cathy.
At fifteen years old, she was the queen of the countryside, the most beautiful girl around.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right two-panel split, ratio 1:2 (left panel smaller as secondary panel, right panel larger as main). Left panel: camera at the main entrance from the interior, angled 45 degrees inward at the doorway, mild downward angle; the stone entrance door frame fills the left foreground edge; the courtyard beyond the door is visible but empty — no curate, no decent visitors, emphasizing absence. Right panel: camera positioned at the right side of the room, angled 30 degrees left-of-front, medium shot looking toward Catherine standing in the center of the room near the fireplace; the wooden hard chair back is visible at the left foreground edge, the stone fireplace arch is visible in the background right; Edgar Linton stands slightly behind Catherine in the midground-right, having entered through the main door. Per-panel budget: left 0 recognizable (empty entrance, absence suggested), right 2 recognizable (Catherine + Edgar).

Left panel: empty stone entrance doorway, no figure approaching, the heavy stone door frame and courtyard visible — the visual emptiness absorbs the sentence about the curate and decent visitors staying away. Right panel: Catherine stands at center on the pale stone floor, posture upright and self-assured, hands lightly holding her dress skirt at her sides, chin slightly raised with an expression of quiet proud beauty; a crown simple sketch floats above her head as a thought-icon identifying her as the queen of the countryside. Edgar Linton stands behind her and slightly to her right, angled facing her direction, expression attentive and admiring.

Wuthering Heights main hall, daytime bright window light; left panel: the stone entrance door frame is the dominant element, pale stone courtyard beyond; right panel: Catherine stands on the pale stone floor one step in front of the fireplace area, the stone fireplace arch is visible approximately at her height in the background, the oak dresser with tin plates visible at the far left background edge; warm daylight from the deep narrow stone windows.

Warm diffuse daylight; right panel slightly warmer at the fireplace zone, left panel slightly cooler at the entrance facing outside. Overall palette bright and warm, reflecting the characters' social prominence.

Catherine Earnshaw (aristocratic young lady period): deep bright eyes, dark brown mid-length hair neatly arranged, slender figure, light or cream-colored fine dress with delicate pleats reaching to the ankle; standing in right panel — her head top reaches approximately the height of the fireplace mantelpiece visible in the background (mantelpiece at approximately adult waist height, Catherine's head at approximately two-thirds adult height); the door frame in the left panel is clearly taller than her figure. Edgar Linton: light golden long curly hair, large calm light-blue eyes, refined elegant face, light-colored gentleman coat, white shirt; standing behind Catherine to her right — his head top visible above her right shoulder, his overall height roughly that of a standard adult male; the stone fireplace arch clearly above both their heads. Proportion anchor: Catherine's head at approximately fireplace mantelpiece height in the background; Edgar's head top visible above Catherine's right shoulder, door frame clearly above both.

Character appearance, clothing details, and scene furniture layout strictly follow reference images; no self-added or modified details; character proportions consistent throughout. White thin straight dividing line between panels, no rounded corners, no shadow, no gradient, no gap; no outer border. Scene fingerprint: large stone-framed fireplace with iron grate + oak dresser with tin plates + pale stone floor, position and form unchanged. Catherine (aristocratic period): hair must not change; light cream dress must not change to dark; hair must not become disheveled; must not change to adult tall proportions. Edgar Linton: light golden long curly hair must not change to dark or straight; light gentleman coat must not change to dark; eyes must not change to dark; no beard or stubble. Proportion anchor: Catherine head at fireplace mantelpiece height; Edgar head above Catherine's shoulder level. No floating text, no narration text, no title text, no label text in the image.

covered_sentences: But she had become proud and bad-tempered.
I admit I did not like her at that time.
She still liked me, though, she was always loyal to her old friends.
And young Linton, despite his superiority, found it difficult to make an equally deep impression on her.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right two-panel split, ratio 1:1 (equal weight: Catherine's proud demeanor vs housekeeper's complex reaction carry equal narrative importance). Left panel: camera positioned at the left side of the room, behind the housekeeper's right shoulder at about 45 degrees, looking toward Catherine; the housekeeper's right shoulder back occupies the left foreground of the frame at about one-quarter width; the stone fireplace left wall edge is the only background visible behind Catherine. Right panel: camera positioned at the right side of the room, angled 20 degrees right-of-front, medium-close shot cutting to the housekeeper from chest upward; the oak dresser left edge is partially visible in the right background.

Left panel: Catherine stands before the fireplace area, arms crossed at chest height, chin slightly raised, brow lightly furrowed in a proud displeased expression; above her head a thought bubble contains the mini-icon of a light-golden-curly-haired male head sketch (representing Edgar Linton) with an arrow pointing to a question mark beside it — absorbing the idea that he failed to make a deep impression. Right panel: the housekeeper faces left-panel direction, lips slightly pressed, brows lightly drawn, expression complex — simultaneously critical and recognizing Catherine's old-friend loyalty; a small speech bubble above her contains a simple heart sketch next to the words "old friends" (direct original text extract) indicating acknowledgment of Catherine's loyalty.

Wuthering Heights main hall, daytime soft light; left panel: Catherine stands on the pale stone floor in front of the fireplace zone, the stone fireplace arch edge is the background behind her, stone floor visible in foreground — Catherine's head top approximately level with the fireplace mantelpiece height in the left background; right panel: the housekeeper stands in the right side of the room, the oak dresser's left edge visible in her right background, pale stone floor at her feet.

Warm indoor daylight throughout; left panel slightly warmer near the fireplace zone; right panel more neutral with the wooden dresser providing a warm-brown background contrast. Overall palette warm-toned but emotionally complex.

Catherine Earnshaw (aristocratic young lady period): deep bright eyes, dark brown mid-length hair neatly arranged, light or cream-colored fine dress with delicate pleats reaching to the ankle; standing in left panel with arms crossed — head top at approximately fireplace mantelpiece height in background (mantelpiece at roughly adult waist height); her figure occupies approximately two-thirds of the left panel height. Housekeeper: deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face with complex expression, dark domestic work dress with white apron, tied collar; in right panel medium-close frame from chest upward, her head and upper body filling approximately two-thirds of panel height; the oak dresser left edge approximately level with her head top as proportion reference. Proportion anchor: Catherine head at fireplace mantelpiece height; housekeeper head approximately level with the oak dresser top edge visible in the right background.

covered_sentences: Do you see that portrait over the fireplace?
That was Edgar Linton, my late master.
He used to hang on one side, and his wife's portrait hung on the other side.
Mrs. Dean raised her candle and went to the fireplace.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right two-panel split, ratio 1:2 (left panel smaller, right panel larger as main). Right panel: camera positioned at the right side of the Thrushcross Grange drawing room, low angle about 15 degrees upward, looking across the room toward the housekeeper walking toward the fireplace; Lockwood's right shoulder back occupies the right foreground of the frame at about one-fifth width as over-shoulder framing, the housekeeper is the midground main subject moving toward the fireplace, and the oval gold-framed portrait above the fireplace is the visual terminus in the upper background. Left panel: camera at medium-close range, angled 20 degrees right-of-front, cutting to Lockwood from chest upward; his gaze directed upward-right toward the right panel direction; only the dark heavy curtain fabric edge is visible in the far background.

Right panel: the housekeeper walks across the deep red carpet toward the fireplace, right hand raised holding a lit candle, left hand slightly lifting her skirt; her gaze is directed upward toward the oval gold-framed portrait above the marble fireplace; the portrait (oval gold frame, male subject) is clearly visible above the mantelpiece; to its left or right the wall shows a faint rectangular outline where a second portrait has been removed — an empty pale rectangle on the wallpaper indicating the absent wife's portrait; a speech bubble from the housekeeper contains the word "removed" (direct text extract from original). Left panel: Lockwood stands near a red upholstered chair, right hand lightly resting on the chair back at his waist height, gaze directed upward-right toward the right panel, expression attentive and curious; no speech bubble.

Thrushcross Grange drawing room, evening or night interior, candlelight; right panel: the housekeeper walks on the deep red carpet, the ornate marble fireplace mantelpiece is at approximately her waist height, the oval gold-framed portrait is on the wall above the mantelpiece, the faint rectangular shadow of the removed second portrait is visible to its side; the housekeeper's head top is approximately at the height of the portrait frame's bottom edge; glass chandelier and heavy curtains are visible in the upper background; left panel: red upholstered chair with chair back at Lockwood's waist height, dark heavy curtain edge at right background.

Evening indoor warm candlelight; right panel: warm orange-amber glow from the candle in the housekeeper's hand illuminating her face and the portrait above, fireplace embers providing secondary warmth from below; left panel: cooler ambient drawing room light with softer gold tone. Overall palette: warm amber and deep red from the carpet, dark walls.

Housekeeper: deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round face with a serious narrating expression, dark domestic dress with white apron, right hand holding a lit candle at chest-to-head height; walking toward the fireplace — standing height consistent with an adult woman, fireplace mantelpiece at her waist height, head top approximately one adult head-height above the mantelpiece, portrait frame bottom edge approximately level with her head top. Lockwood: light brown or tan short neat hair, medium build, light-colored shirt and dark coat; in left panel medium-close from chest upward, his head and upper body filling approximately two-thirds of the panel height; the chair back at his waist height as proportion reference. Proportion anchor: housekeeper's waist at fireplace mantelpiece height, head top near portrait frame bottom edge; Lockwood's waist at chair back height.

covered_sentences: The delicate features of Edgar Linton's portrait were very much like those of the young lady at Wuthering Heights, but his expression was gentle and thoughtful, very different from hers.
His light hair was long and curly.
His eyes were large and serious.
His features were almost too graceful.
I was not surprised that Catherine Earnshaw could forget her first friend for a man like this.
More surprising was that he, if his mind were as noble and delicate as his person, could fall in love with the kind of woman I imagined her to be.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Main image with circular portrait inset in upper-right corner (circular inset occupies approximately one-tenth of the frame area). Main image: camera positioned at the front-left of the Thrushcross Grange drawing room, low angle about 20 degrees upward, looking toward the housekeeper and fireplace from Lockwood's left shoulder over-shoulder position; Lockwood's left shoulder back occupies the left foreground at about one-sixth width; the housekeeper stands at the fireplace holding a candle as the midground main subject; the oval gold-framed portrait above the fireplace is the visual focal point in the upper background. Circular inset in upper-right: a close-up view of the portrait painting face — light golden long curly hair, large calm eyes, refined graceful features — contained within a thin oval gold frame ring. Per-panel budget: main image 2 recognizable figures (housekeeper + Lockwood); portrait inset is a painted artwork prop, not a real character render, not counted in character budget.

Main image: the housekeeper stands before the fireplace, right hand holding the candle raised to chest-to-head height, candle flame illuminating her face and the portrait above; her gaze fixed on the oval portrait, expression thoughtful and retrospective; a thought bubble from the housekeeper floats toward the upper-left, containing two female mini-icon sketches side by side — left: a dark-haired disheveled young girl head sketch (representing young wild Catherine) and right: a neatly-dressed refined young lady head sketch (representing the older Catherine) — with the words "first friend" (direct text extract) below them, absorbing the reflective comparison. Lockwood stands behind the housekeeper to her left, his gaze also directed upward toward the portrait, right hand lightly touching his chin in a thoughtful pose; no speech bubble. Circular inset upper-right: portrait face only, light golden long curly hair, large serious light-blue eyes, almost too-graceful refined features, thin oval gold frame ring.

Thrushcross Grange drawing room, night interior, candlelight; main image: the housekeeper stands directly before the ornate marble fireplace, the mantelpiece at her waist height, the oval gold-framed portrait centered above it on the wall; the wall beside the portrait shows the faint rectangular outline of the removed wife's portrait; fireplace embers glow faintly from below; the deep red carpet extends to the foreground; red upholstered chairs and the glass chandelier are visible in the peripheral background; Lockwood stands to the housekeeper's left rear, the red chair back at his waist height.

Night interior dual light source: the candle in the housekeeper's right hand produces a warm orange-amber glow that illuminates her face upward and highlights the portrait above; the fireplace embers provide a secondary warm red-orange light from below. The circular portrait inset is rendered slightly brighter than the ambient room, as if spotlit by the candle reaching it. Overall palette: deep warm amber, dark red carpet, shadowed walls.

Housekeeper: deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round reflective face, dark domestic dress with white apron, tied collar, right hand holding lit candle; standing before fireplace — mantelpiece at her waist height, head top approximately one head-height above the mantelpiece, portrait frame bottom edge near her head top level. Lockwood: light brown or tan short neat hair, medium build, light shirt and dark coat; standing behind housekeeper to her left — head top approximately at the same height as the housekeeper; the chair back visible at his waist height as proportion reference. Proportion anchor: housekeeper waist at fireplace mantelpiece height; Lockwood and housekeeper at approximately equal standing height; chair back at both characters' waist height.
